LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF KAZUKO ARTUS' MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEEMENT IS DENIED. THE COURT DENIES THE MOTION AS MOOT AS TO PARAGRAPH G OF THE SETTLEMENT BECAUSE DEFENDANT DID RETAIN ATTORNEY PEARSON TO MAKE AN INQUIRY WITH THE IRS. THE COURT DENIES THE MOTION AS TO PARAGRAPH F OF THE SETTLEMENT. DEFENDANT COMPLIED WITH THAT COVENANT BY OBTAINING THE MEMBERS' RATIFICATION REGARDING THE 2008 - 2013 ASSESSMENTS AND THE PARAGRAPH DOES NOT APPLY PROSPECTIVELY TO 2014. THE COURT DENIES WITHOUT PREJUDICE AS TO PARAGRAPH E. IT APPEARS THAT DEFENDANT HAS DRAFTED THE REQUIRED MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT AND IF A FULLY EXECUTED DOCUMENT IS NOT CREATED SHORTLY, THEN PLAINTIFF MAY RENEW THIS MOTION. THE COURT DENIES AS TO THE FEE ISSUE UNDER PARAGRAPH I BECAUSE DEFENDANT MADE THE PAYMENT. THE COURT DENIES AS TO THE BOOKKEEPER FEE INSTRUCTIONS UNDER PARAGRAPH I BECAUSE DEFENDANT COMPLIED WITH THE SETTLEMENT. PLAINTIFF MAY FILE A MOTION TO RECOVER FEES UNDER THE SETTLEMENT BECAUSE THIS MOTION WAS A NECESSARY IMPETUS TO OBTAIN COMPLIANCE WITH THE PARTIES' AGREEMENT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR5976

Notice Of Motion To Enforce
Off Calendar Jan-14-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via ZOOM Video-conferencing:

Attorney, David J. Millstein (Email: dmillstein@millstein-law.com), of the law offices of Millstein & Associates (Tel: 415.348.0348) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Al (Albert) Flor (Email: aflor@wendel.com), of the law offices of Wendel, Rosen, Black & Dean LLP (Tel: 510.834.6600) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

on calendar:
PLAINTIFF KAZUKO ARTUS' Motion To Enforce

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides on record as reflected in the notes of the Certified Shorthand Reporter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Kazuko K. Artus's motion to enforce this Court's November 24, 2014 order is denied as moot in substantial part. The primary relief Plaintiff seeks is an order compelling the Defendant Association to return the 2019 Operating Surplus to Association members. (See Mot. at 4:17 ["This motion is to compel Association to return the excess funds immediately."]; id. at 6:25-26 ["Plaintiff requests this Court to compel the Defendant to return the 2019 Operating Accounting Surplus immediately."].) However, Defendant shows that the 2019 operating surplus was returned to GTCA members on a pro rata basis on November 1, 2020 by the management company at the direction of the Board. (Gordon Decl., par. 14.) "[A]n injunction serves to prevent future injury and is not applicable to wrongs that have been completed. An injunction is authorized only when it appears that wrongful acts are likely to recur." (Russell v. Douvan (2003) 112 Cal.App.4th 399, 402.) Further, while Defendant admits that certain technical violations may have occurred, Plaintiff does not show that contempt of court or other extraordinary relief is warranted.

The Court does not believe that its prior order, entered more than six years ago, provides a sufficient basis for it to summarily adjudicate by motion the parties' remaining disputes, nor does Plaintiff provide authority that would authorize the Court to intervene in the dispute and impose the detailed remedies requested without a full evidentiary hearing, which this department is not in a position to provide. If Plaintiff alleges that the underlying settlement agreement has been breached, she may file a supplemental complaint or file a separate action. Alternatively, if either party believes that appointment of a special master would assist them in resolving their numerous disputes, they may stipulate to such appointment or bring a separate motion seeking such relief.

Written order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com.

Mtn To Enforce Settlement Agreeement
Off Calendar Jan-23-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: DAVID MILLSTEIN, ESQ., 415.348.0348, FOR PLAINTIFF KUZUKO ARTUS.
GEORGE ZISER, ESQ., 415.362.2580, FOR DEFENDANT GRAMERCY TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF KAZUKO ARTUS' Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreeement is denied. The Court denies the motion as moot as to Paragraph G of the settlement because defendant did retain attorney Pearson to make an inquiry with the IRS. The Court denies the motion as to paragraph F of the settlement. Defendant complied with that covenant by obtaining the members' ratification regarding the 2008 - 2013 assessments and the paragraph does not apply prospectively to 2014. The Court denies without prejudice as to paragraph E. It appears that defendant has drafted the required management agreement and if a fully executed document is not created shortly, then plaintiff may renew this motion. The Court denies as to the fee issue under paragraph I because defendant made the payment. The Court denies as to the bookkeeper fee instructions under paragraph I because defendant complied with the settlement. Plaintiff may file a motion to recover fees under the settlement because this motion was a necessary impetus to obtain compliance with the parties' agreement. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976
